title: Evidence
caption: By evening, the bakery had quietly transformed from a place that sold bread into something warmer — a gathering spot, a living room without the living room. Detective Park was holding court at the centre table, manila folder open, surveillance photos fanned out like a hand of cards. The case: Mrs. Petrov's tabby had been raiding Mr. Okonkwo's recycling bins for three weeks running. The photos were blurry. The detective was not deterred. 'The perpetrator was operating between 11 PM and 2 AM,' he announced. 'Classic pattern.' Marco said, from the doorway, 'It's Mrs. Petrov's cat, Detective.' Park waved the folder. 'The HOW is irrelevant. What matters is I have EVIDENCE.' The table laughed — the older woman nearly spilled her tea, the cyclist knocked his helmet off his knee. Chloe laughed too, and only afterwards noticed that she had. It was the first time since leaving. She hadn't realized she'd stopped.
story_notes: Chloe laughs for the first time since arriving, watching Detective Park present evidence on a cat theft case to the bakery regulars.
